slides.title: Good olive oil has a *natural anti-inflammatory* in it.
slides.sub: It's called oleocanthal — and it acts a little like ibuprofen. That peppery sting is the tell.

slides.title: The peppery kick
slides.body: Oleocanthal is what makes fresh olive oil *sting the back of your throat*. That burn is the active part.

slides.title: Calms inflammation
slides.body: It works on the *same pathway as ibuprofen* — gently, over time, not as a painkiller dose.

slides.title: Slow inflammation ages you
slides.body: Chronic low-grade inflammation is tied to *heart and brain aging*. Food-level help adds up.

slides.title: Look for the sting
slides.body: Choose *extra-virgin in a dark bottle*, recent harvest date. A peppery finish means the good stuff survived.

slides.title: Pour it raw
slides.body: Drizzle it *over finished food* — salads, veg, soup. Less heat keeps more of the compound intact.

slides.title: Small daily swaps that *quietly compound*.
slides.body: Vibe Health logs your meals and turns your food, sleep, and movement into one daily score — so the small upgrades actually show up.

caption:
That peppery sting in good olive oil? It's a compound called oleocanthal. 🫒

It nudges the same inflammation pathway as ibuprofen — gently, over time. Buy extra-virgin in a dark bottle, look for a recent harvest, and pour it over finished food. 🔖

Vibe Health turns small food upgrades into one daily score → link in bio.
youtube_title: The Anti-Inflammatory Hiding in Your Olive Oil
